signature_questions.what_they_press_on: Asks how new products/destinations (Utopia, Icon, Perfect Day, Royal Beach Club, Perfect Day Mexico) translate to demand, yields and long‑term TAM capture; probes cadence and ramp effects.
Frequently asks how RCL is balancing pricing power versus multi‑year market‑share capture from land‑based vacations and the $1.9–$2.0 trillion TAM.
Seeks clarity on percent booked, optimal booking curve, whether they are 'booked where they want to be' and how that translates into yield optimization.

topics.what_they_press_on: Asks how new products/destinations (Utopia, Icon, Perfect Day, Royal Beach Club, Perfect Day Mexico) translate to demand, yields and long‑term TAM capture; probes cadence and ramp effects.
Frequently asks how RCL is balancing pricing power versus multi‑year market‑share capture from land‑based vacations and the $1.9–$2.0 trillion TAM.
Seeks clarity on percent booked, optimal booking curve, whether they are 'booked where they want to be' and how that translates into yield optimization.
Asks about new‑to‑cruise trends, loyalty initiatives (Points Choice), and whether mix shifts (first‑time cruisers) are durable.

distinctive_concerns.blind_spots: No evidence in the history provided of questions on fuel‑hedging or bunker hedges (0 turns across 12 calls). He also rarely drills into operational/port logistics (berthing rights) or detailed share‑repurchase mechanics; capital return mechanics / timing are not a recurring ask (n_turns < 2).
distinctive_concerns.stance_drift: Early in the sample (2023‑Q1/2023‑Q2) he focused heavily on Trifecta, like‑for‑like yield drivers and margin ceilings; by 2024‑Q4/2025‑Q1 his questions shift toward market share, portfolio differentiation and investment runway (new destinations and tech). The emphasis moved from 'are we back to 2019 levels?' (2023-Q2) to 'what inning are we in on TAM capture?' (2024-Q4 / 2025-Q1).

coverage_profile.seniority_signal: Title shown repeatedly as Managing Director and Senior Equity Research Analyst (metadata and multiple turns). Management addresses him informally (e.g., "Thanks, Matt"), consistent with a senior, ongoing coverage relationship. He appears as a repeat, white‑boarded asker across 12 distinct RCL calls.
coverage_profile.sector_lens: Covers cruise/leisure/travel with a consumer-facing, experience‑led lens — repeatedly asks about bookings, yields, onboard spend, private destinations (Perfect Day/Nassau), and market share versus land‑based vacations. Questions regularly bridge revenue dynamics and multi‑year strategic investments.
rhetorical_signature: opens with "Great, thanks, and congrats on another nice quarter." — seen in multiple calls (e.g., 2023-Q1, 2023-Q2, 2023-Q3, 2023-Q4, 2024-Q1, 2024-Q2, 2025-Q1, 2025-Q2)
tends to ask multi-part, framing questions as 'Maybe/Maybe to follow up...' and closes with 'Congrats' or 'Thanks.'
tone: constructive, affirming opener then detailed, model‑oriented drilling; frequently uses phrases like 'how best to think about' or 'maybe could you elaborate'

reasoning_style.primary_mode_justification: Questions combine quantitative asks (EBITDA margins, APCD, % cost guidance, booked position) with strategic probes (market share, Perfect Day, Trifecta, product rollouts) across calls (e.g., 2023-Q2, 2023-Q4, 2025-Q1).
reasoning_style.follow_up_pattern: Multi-part questions are the norm (≈70% of his turns are multi‑part, asking 2+ subquestions); he frequently follows up in a subsequent turn to clarify guidance or push on cost math. He pushes back occasionally but more often seeks reconciliation or incremental detail.
reasoning_style.evidence_demanded: He consistently asks for specific numbers and guidance (EBITDA margin targets, APCD, '% cost guide', booked position). Example: asked about the '4% cost guide' (2023-Q4) and '200-300 basis points above 2019' (2024-Q4). n_turns with explicit numeric asks: many (≈15+ turns) across the sample.
reasoning_style.anchoring_habits: He anchors to management's prior frameworks and milestones (the 'Trifecta' plan — 2023-Q1/2023-Q2/2023-Q4), the 2019 baseline (asks 'relative to 2019' — 2023-Q2/2023-Q3), and management's just‑delivered phrases like 'booked position' and the '4% cost guide' (2023-Q4). He rarely frames questions primarily around peers, though he will ask market‑share comparisons to land‑based alternatives (2024-Q1/2024-Q4).

queue_behavior.typical_position: #3–#5 of the queue on most calls; he was #1 on 3 of 33 observed turns (2025-Q1, 2025-Q2, 2025-Q4) and appeared as an early questioner across the sample.
queue_behavior.default_first_pick: When first or early to speak he opens on demand/booking momentum and strategic initiatives that drive durable share (e.g., asked drivers of Q1 outperformance and company investments in 2025-Q1; asked about demand acceleration and playbook in 2025-Q2; asked 2026 acceleration/portfolio differentiation in 2025-Q4).

adaptation_when_favorites_taken.reframe_deeper: Yes — he often drills deeper on the same topic (example: 2023-Q2 asked about 'EBITDA margins multi-year' after others discussed demand).
adaptation_when_favorites_taken.cross_narrative_reconcile: Yes — he asks management to reconcile threads raised earlier (example: 2023-Q3 asked about 'balancing pricing power relative to the multi‑year market share opportunity').
adaptation_when_favorites_taken.reactive_followup: Yes — he explicitly builds on management phrases (example: 2023-Q1 referenced the 'Trifecta plan' in his follow-up).
adaptation_when_favorites_taken.stay_on_topic_anyway: Yes — he will re‑ask popular topics regardless (example: repeatedly returns to 'relative to the Trifecta plan' across 2023–2024).

supporting_evidence_phrases.quote: balancing pricing power relative to the multi-year market share opportunity

explicit_peer_references.note: No evidence in the provided history of Matthew explicitly prefacing a question with another analyst's name or 'following up on X's question'.

management_line_anchoring.note: He frequently anchors questions to management's just‑stated slogans, guidance bands and constructs (Trifecta, Wave, 'booked position', '% cost guide').

cross_analyst_reactivity.operator_or_followup_signal: He often takes a second turn when allowed to clarify guidance or press on modeling (examples: followed initial Q with a Naftali guidance drill in 2025-Q1 and pursued expanded assumption questions in 2025-Q2). When he takes followups they are used for clarification or to reconcile prior answers rather than to open a wholly new topic.

framing_overrides_by_theme.booking_dynamics: Asks for booked-position color tied to margin and yield optimization.
framing_overrides_by_theme.yield_decomposition: Asks for durable multi-year yield drivers in bps relative to pre-pandemic margin baseline.

_built_from: v7: company-layer split + probe/topic structure + v6 prose
